summary:
Myasthenia gravis involves problems at the neuromuscular junction due to autoimmune attacks on nAChR or MuSK; symptoms include fluctuating muscle weakness which improves with rest. This condition impacts how signals from nerves reach muscles leading to their dysfunction. To summarize:

Myasthenia gravis disrupts nerve-muscle communication via impaired neuromuscular junction function caused by antibody-mediated damage, resulting in variable muscular fatigue alleviated by periods of repose. Key aspects are fluctuations in severity based on activity levels and presence of specific receptor-targeted immune responses. ```plaintext
Myasthenia gravis impairs signal transmission at the neuromuscular junction throughautoimmune attack on nAChR or MuSK, causing fluctuating muscle weakness relievedby resting. 
```
